headline: Mbappé, the French team's adored and criticized superstar
description: The captain of Les Bleus, who begin their quest for a third World Cup title on Tuesday, boasts impressive statistics for both club and national. However, in recent years, his public image has declined.

'On the ground, it's a disaster': In Bunia, Democratic Republic of Congo, Ebola epidemic spirals out of control
description: One month after the Congolese authorities officially declared the outbreak, the health response is failing to contain the virus's spread, and the number of victims continues to rise.

headline: Paris's BHV department store drops Shein after ownership change
description: SGM, which has operated the landmark store opposite Paris City Hall since 2023, has sold it at a loss to a group of executives, including outgoing SGM CEO Karl-Stephane Cottendin. He said tha Shein would 'ideally' exit the store by Christmas.

headline: Spielberg's 'Disclosure Day' aliens have nothing to do with Trump's
description: Days before the film was released worldwide, the White House co-opted the imagery of 'aliens' to further its rhetoric and anti-immigration policy.

headline: Why AI systems, which rely on the internet, poorly reflect the diversity of human knowledge
description: As the internet supplies the vast amounts of data AI systems need for training, ChatGPT and the likes inherit the web's biases and thus cannot claim to be comprehensive.

description: As his second term grows increasingly complex at home amid the killing of 11-year-old Lyhanna, the French president, who is chairing the G7 summit in Evian, is staking his legitimacy on an ambitious international agenda.

headline: At Dior, Jonathan Anderson is thinking long-term, not 'trendy'
description: One year after this arrival at Dior, the 41-year-old Northern Irish designer is still going at full throttle, releasing collections for Uniqlo and his own brand JW Anderson in addition to his 10 for the luxury label. His goal, he says, is to 'transform' the brand.
